fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Neural Ordinary Differential Equations

fr:mainmatter.html:p: Variational Inference with Normalizing Flows, Neural Ordinary Differential Equations

fr:tree.fr:mainmatter: Since we are considering probability measures over \mathbb {R}^n, it is not enough to just define a mapping between spaces. Explicitly, say that we are considering the two probability measures p \sim \mathrm {Unif}[0,1] and q \sim \mathrm {Unif}[0,2]. Trivially, we have the mapping f : [0, 1] \to [0,2] given by f(x) \mapsto 2x.
